samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating multiplication is done. Shift count register, 0 to 16. Update shift count register. if (~rst_n) i <= 0; else if (start && i < 17) i <= i + 1; else if (!start) i <= 0; Update completion flag. if (~rst_n) done_r <= 0; else if (i == 16) done_r <= 1; else if (i == 17) done_r <= 0; Update product register. if (~rst_n) yout_r <= 0; else if (start) begin if (i == 0) begin areg <= ain; breg <= bin; end else if (i > 0 && i < 17 && areg[i-1]) yout_r <= yout_r + (breg << (i-1)); end The module performs shift and accumulate operations to compute the product of the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin). The multiplication process is initiated by asserting the start signal. The product output (yout) is ready when the done signal is asserted. The module supports multiple cycles for the multiplication operation based on the bit position of the multiplicand. endmodule
